briefdescription.para: The shape of the array {ni, nj}.
The underlying data storage, a vector of size shape.x * shape.y.
Constructs a new Array object.

parameterdescription.para: The shape of the array {ni, nj}.
The initial value to fill the array with.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Overloads the assignment operator for scalar assignment.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to assign.

simplesect.para: Array& Reference to the current Array object.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the multiplication-assignment operator for scalar multiplication.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to multiply.

simplesect.para: Array& Reference to the current Array object.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Overloads the division-assignment operator for scalar division.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to divide by.

simplesect.para: Array& Reference to the current Array object.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Overloads the addition-assignment operator for scalar addition.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to add.

simplesect.para: Array& Reference to the current Array object.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Overloads the subtraction-assignment operator for scalar subtraction.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to subtract.

simplesect.para: Array& Reference to the current Array object.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Overloads the multiplication operator for scalar multiplication.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to multiply.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after multiplication.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the multiplication operator for element-wise multiplication with another array.

parameterdescription.para: The Array to multiply with.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after element-wise multiplication.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the division operator for scalar division.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to divide by.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after division.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the division operator for element-wise division by another array.

parameterdescription.para: The Array to divide by.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after element-wise division.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the addition operator for scalar addition.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to add.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after addition.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the addition operator for element-wise addition with another array.

parameterdescription.para: The Array to add.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after element-wise addition.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the unary minus operator.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after applying the unary minus.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the subtraction operator for scalar subtraction.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to subtract.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after subtraction.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the subtraction operator for element-wise subtraction with another array.

parameterdescription.para: The Array to subtract.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after element-wise subtraction.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the function call operator to access the array value at index (i, j).

parameterdescription.para: The row index.
The column index.

simplesect.para: float& Reference to the array value at index (i, j).

briefdescription.para: Overloads the function call operator to access the array value at index (i, j) (const version).

parameterdescription.para: The row index.
The column index.

simplesect.para: const float& Reference to the array value at index (i, j). <This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the function call operator to access the array value at the linear index 'index' (const version).

parameterdescription.para: Linear index.

simplesect.para: const float& Reference to the array value. <This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the function call operator to access the array value at the linear index 'index'.

parameterdescription.para: Linear index.

simplesect.para: float& Reference to the array value.

briefdescription.para: Finds the maximum value in the array and its coordinates.

parameterdescription.para: The maximum value found.
The x-index of the maximum value.
The y-index of the maximum value.

briefdescription.para: Extracts a column 'j' as a std::vector.

parameterdescription.para: The column index.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> The column values as a vector.

briefdescription.para: Return the number of non-zero elements in the array.

simplesect.para: int The number of non-zero elements.

memberdef.argsstring: (int i, int j, float u, float v, float amount)
memberdef.name: depose_amount_bilinear_at

briefdescription.para: Distribute a value 'amount' around the four cells (i, j), (i + 1, j), (i, j + 1), (i + 1, j + 1) by "reversing" the bilinear interpolation.

parameterdescription.para: Index.
Index.
'u' interpolation parameter, expected to be in [0, 1[.
'v' interpolation parameter, expected to be in [0, 1[.
Amount to be deposited.

memberdef.argsstring: (int i, int j, float u, float v, int ir, float amount)
memberdef.name: depose_amount_kernel_bilinear_at

memberdef.argsstring: (int i, int j, const Array &kernel, float amount)
memberdef.name: depose_amount_kernel_at

briefdescription.para: Distributes a specified amount of value around the cell located at (i, j) using a 1D deposition kernel applied in both horizontal and vertical directions.

parameterdescription.para: Index of the cell in the array where the deposition starts.
Index of the cell in the array where the deposition starts.
1D deposition kernel to be used for distribution. The kernel must contain an odd number of elements.
The amount of value to be distributed around the cell.

briefdescription.para: Debug tool, dump some infos and generate an output file (16bits grayscale)
Debug tool, dump array content as an ASCII histogram to the consol.
Extracts a subarray defined by the slice indices {i1, i2, j1, j2} from the original array, creating a new array. Note that i2 and j2 are excluded from the slice.

parameterdescription.para: A glm::ivec4 containing the slice extent indices {i1, i2, j1, j2}.

simplesect.para: Array The extracted subarray.

memberdef.argsstring: (int i1, int i2, int j1, int j2) const
memberdef.name: extract_slice

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Import array data from a raw binary file.

detaileddescription.para: This function loads the array data from a binary file specified by fname. The file should contain raw binary data that corresponds to the internal representation of the array. The array's size and data type must match the expected format in the file.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the file to import data from.

briefdescription.para: Import array data from a numpy binary file.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the file to import data from.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the gradient in the 'x' (or 'i') direction at a specified index (i, j) using a 2nd order central difference scheme.

detaileddescription.para: This function computes the gradient in the x-direction, which measures the rate of change along the x-axis. Note that this function cannot be used at the borders of the data, as the central difference scheme requires values from both sides of the point of interest.

simplesect.para: The gradient cannot be computed at the borders of the data array. Specifically, the function is not defined for i = 0, j = 0, i = shape.x - 1, or j = shape.y - 1.

parameterdescription.para: Index along the x-direction, expected to be in the range [1, shape.x - 2].
Index along the y-direction, expected to be in the range [1, shape.y - 2].

simplesect.para: float The computed gradient value in the x-direction at the specified index.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the gradient in the 'y' (or 'j') direction at a specified index (i, j) using a 2nd order central difference scheme.

detaileddescription.para: This function computes the gradient in the y-direction, which measures the rate of change along the y-axis. Note that this function cannot be used at the borders of the data, as the central difference scheme requires values from both sides of the point of interest.

simplesect.para: The gradient cannot be computed at the borders of the data array. Specifically, the function is not defined for i = 0, j = 0, i = shape.x - 1, or j = shape.y - 1.

parameterdescription.para: Index along the x-direction, expected to be in the range [1, shape.x - 2].
Index along the y-direction, expected to be in the range [1, shape.y - 2].

simplesect.para: float The computed gradient value in the y-direction at the specified index.

memberdef.argsstring: (int i, int j, float u, float v) const
memberdef.name: get_gradient_x_bilinear_at

briefdescription.para: Calculates the gradient in the 'x' (or 'i') direction at a location (x, y) near the index (i, j) using bilinear interpolation.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses bilinear interpolation to estimate the gradient in the x-direction at a point within the cell defined by (i, j). The gradient is based on a 2nd order central difference scheme but interpolated to a finer resolution.

simplesect.para: The gradient cannot be computed at the borders of the data array. Specifically, the function is not defined for i = 0, j = 0, i = shape.x - 1, or j = shape.y - 1.

parameterdescription.para: Index along the x-direction, expected to be in the range [1, shape.x - 2].
Index along the y-direction, expected to be in the range [1, shape.y - 2].
'u' interpolation parameter, expected to be in the range [0, 1[.
'v' interpolation parameter, expected to be in the range [0, 1[.

simplesect.para: float The interpolated gradient value in the x-direction at the specified location.

memberdef.argsstring: (int i, int j, float u, float v) const
memberdef.name: get_gradient_y_bilinear_at

briefdescription.para: Calculates the gradient in the 'y' (or 'j') direction at a location (x, y) near the index (i, j) using bilinear interpolation.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses bilinear interpolation to estimate the gradient in the y-direction at a point within the cell defined by (i, j). The gradient is based on a 2nd order central difference scheme but interpolated to a finer resolution.

simplesect.para: The gradient cannot be computed at the borders of the data array. Specifically, the function is not defined for i = 0, j = 0, i = shape.x - 1, or j = shape.y - 1.

parameterdescription.para: Index along the x-direction, expected to be in the range [1, shape.x - 2].
Index along the y-direction, expected to be in the range [1, shape.y - 2].
'u' interpolation parameter, expected to be in the range [0, 1[.
'v' interpolation parameter, expected to be in the range [0, 1[.

simplesect.para: float The interpolated gradient value in the y-direction at the specified location.

briefdescription.para: Calculates the surface normal vector at the index (i, j).

detaileddescription.para: This function computes the surface normal at the specified index (i, j) in the data array. The normal vector is a three-dimensional vector (x, y, z) that is perpendicular to the surface at that point.

parameterdescription.para: Index along the x-direction.
Index along the y-direction.

simplesect.para: glm::vec3 The normal vector at the specified index (i, j).

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the shape of the array.

simplesect.para: glm::ivec2 The shape {ni, nj}.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the number of bytes occupied by the array data.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns the total size in bytes of the underlying storage vector that holds the array data. This can be useful for memory management or debugging purposes.

simplesect.para: size_t The size of the array data in bytes.

memberdef.argsstring: (int i, int j, float u, float v) const
memberdef.name: get_value_bicubic_at

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the array value at the location (x, y) near the index (i, j) using bicubic interpolation.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses bicubic interpolation to estimate the value at a point within the cell defined by (i, j). Bicubic interpolation provides a smoother result compared to bilinear interpolation by taking into account the values of the surrounding 4x4 grid of points, resulting in better accuracy when interpolating in areas with gradients or curves.

simplesect.para: This function cannot be used at the upper borders of the array, specifically for i = shape.x - 2 or j = shape.y - 2, as bicubic interpolation requires surrounding data points from a 4x4 neighborhood.

parameterdescription.para: Index along the x-direction, expected to be in the range [1, shape.x - 3].
Index along the y-direction, expected to be in the range [1, shape.y - 3].
'u' interpolation parameter, expected to be in the range [0, 1[.
'v' interpolation parameter, expected to be in the range [0, 1[.

simplesect.para: float The interpolated value at the specified location (x, y).

memberdef.argsstring: (int i, int j, float u, float v) const
memberdef.name: get_value_bilinear_at

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the array value at the location (x, y) near the index (i, j) using bilinear interpolation.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses bilinear interpolation to estimate the value at a point within the cell defined by (i, j). Bilinear interpolation is applied based on the surrounding values to approximate the value at the specified (x, y) location.

simplesect.para: This function cannot be used at the upper borders of the array, specifically for i = shape.x - 1 or j = shape.y - 1, as bilinear interpolation requires surrounding data points.

parameterdescription.para: Index along the x-direction, expected to be in the range [0, shape.x - 2].
Index along the y-direction, expected to be in the range [0, shape.y - 2].
'u' interpolation parameter, expected to be in the range [0, 1[.
'v' interpolation parameter, expected to be in the range [0, 1[.

simplesect.para: float The interpolated value at the specified location (x, y).

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the nearest value at the location (x, y) within a bounded domain.

detaileddescription.para: This function retrieves the nearest value to the specified (x, y) coordinates from the array, assuming the array covers a domain defined by the bounding box bbox. If the coordinates (x, y) are outside the domain, they are clamped to the nearest valid position within the bounding box.

parameterdescription.para: Coordinate along the x-axis.
Coordinate along the y-axis.
Bounding box defining the domain of the array. It is expected to be in the form of {xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax}.

simplesect.para: float The nearest value at the clamped location (x, y).

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the underlying data vector.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> The vector containing the array's data.

briefdescription.para: Display various information about the array.

detaileddescription.para: This function outputs details about the array, such as its dimensions, data type, and any other relevant information. The optional msg parameter can be used to include a custom message in the output.

parameterdescription.para: Optional message to include in the output.

briefdescription.para: Return the linear index corresponding to the (i, j) cell in a 2D array.

detaileddescription.para: This function calculates the linear index for a cell specified by the (i, j) coordinates in a 2D array. The linear index is useful for accessing elements in a flattened or 1D representation of the array.

parameterdescription.para: The 'i' index (row index).
The 'j' index (column index).

simplesect.para: int The linear index corresponding to the (i, j) cell.

briefdescription.para: Convert a linear index to its corresponding (i, j) cell coordinates.

detaileddescription.para: This function calculates the 2D cell coordinates (i, j) in the array from a given linear index k. This is useful for mapping between the 1D and 2D representations of the array.

parameterdescription.para: The linear index.

simplesect.para: glm::ivec2 The (i, j) coordinates corresponding to the linear index k.

briefdescription.para: Return the value of the greatest element in the array.

detaileddescription.para: This function computes the maximum value present in the array. If the array is empty, the behavior of this function is undefined.

simplesect.para: float The maximum value in the array.

briefdescription.para: Return the mean value of the elements in the array.

detaileddescription.para: This function calculates the average value of all elements in the array. If the array is empty, the behavior of this function is undefined.

simplesect.para: float The mean value of the elements in the array.

briefdescription.para: Return the value of the smallest element in the array.

detaileddescription.para: This function computes the minimum value present in the array. If the array is empty, the behavior of this function is undefined.

simplesect.para: float The minimum value in the array.

briefdescription.para: Normalize array values so that the sum of all elements is equal to 1.

detaileddescription.para: This function adjusts the values in the array so that their total sum is normalized to 1. This is often used in scenarios where the array represents a probability distribution.

briefdescription.para: Computes normalization coefficients (a, b) such that a * array + b maps the values to the range [vmin, vmax].

detaileddescription.para: This function calculates the coefficients used to normalize the array values so that they fit within the specified range [vmin, vmax]. The normalization is performed using the linear transformation: a * array + b.

parameterdescription.para: Lower bound of the desired range.
Upper bound of the desired range.

simplesect.para: glm::vec2 Normalization coefficients (a, b) where a scales the values and b shifts them.

briefdescription.para: Print the array values to the standard output (stdout).

detaileddescription.para: This function prints the contents of the array in a human-readable format to the standard output. This can be useful for debugging or inspecting the values of the array.

briefdescription.para: Return the peak-to-peak amplitude (i.e., the difference between the maximum and minimum values) of the array values.

detaileddescription.para: This function calculates the peak-to-peak amplitude of the array, which is the difference between the maximum and minimum values in the array.

simplesect.para: float The peak-to-peak amplitude of the array values.

briefdescription.para: Computes the minimum and maximum values of the array.

simplesect.para: glm::vec2 A vector where x contains the minimum value and y contains the maximum value.

memberdef.argsstring: (float p_low, float p_high, size_t bins=1024) const
memberdef.name: range_percentile

briefdescription.para: Computes an approximate percentile range using a histogram.

detaileddescription.para: This function estimates the values corresponding to the given lower and upper percentiles by building a histogram over the data range and accumulating counts. The result is an approximation whose precision depends on the number of bins.

parameterdescription.para: Lower percentile in [0, 1]
Upper percentile in [0, 1]
Number of bins used for the histogram (higher = more precise)

simplesect.para: glm::vec2 A vector where x contains the approximated lower percentile value and y contains the approximated upper percentile value

briefdescription.para: Return an array remapped to [0, 1].
Return a resampled array of shape new_shape using bilinear interpolation.

detaileddescription.para: This function resamples the array to a new shape `new_shape` using bilinear interpolation. The resampling changes the dimensions of the array while preserving the data's spatial relationships.

parameterdescription.para: The target shape for the resampled array.

simplesect.para: Array The resampled array with the specified new_shape.

briefdescription.para: Return a resampled array of shape new_shape using bicubic interpolation.

detaileddescription.para: This function resamples the array to a new shape `new_shape` using bicubic interpolation. The resampling changes the dimensions of the array while preserving the data's spatial relationships with a higher degree of smoothness compared to bilinear interpolation.

parameterdescription.para: The target shape for the resampled array.

simplesect.para: Array The resampled array with the specified new_shape.

briefdescription.para: Return a resampled array of shape new_shape using nearest neighbor interpolation.

detaileddescription.para: This function resamples the array to a new shape `new_shape` using nearest neighbor interpolation. This method selects the value from the nearest neighbor in the original array, which can be faster but may produce blocky artifacts compared to other interpolation methods.

parameterdescription.para: The target shape for the resampled array.

simplesect.para: Array The resampled array with the specified new_shape.

briefdescription.para: Return a row i as a std::vector<float>.

detaileddescription.para: This function extracts the specified row `i` from the array and returns it as a std::vector<float>. This is useful for operations where you need to work with individual rows outside of the array structure.

parameterdescription.para: Row index to extract.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> The values of the specified row as a vector.

briefdescription.para: Sets a new shape for the array.

parameterdescription.para: The new shape of the array.

briefdescription.para: Set the value of a slice defined by indices {i1, i2, j1, j2} to a new value.

detaileddescription.para: This function updates all elements within the specified slice of the array to the given value. The slice is defined by the extents {i1, i2, j1, j2}, where i1 and i2 specify the range for the rows, and j1 and j2 specify the range for the columns.

parameterdescription.para: Slice extent indices: {i1, i2, j1, j2}.
The new value to set for the specified slice.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Return the total number of elements in the array.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns the size of the array, which is the total number of elements it contains. This can be useful for iterating over the array or for checking its dimensions.

simplesect.para: int The total number of elements in the array.

briefdescription.para: Return the standard deviation of all array values.

simplesect.para: float The standard deviation of all elements in the array.

briefdescription.para: Return the sum of all array values.

detaileddescription.para: This function calculates and returns the sum of all elements in the array. It can be used to obtain aggregate values or to check the overall magnitude of the array's contents.

simplesect.para: float The sum of all elements in the array.

briefdescription.para: Converts a 2D Array to an OpenCV cv::Mat.

detaileddescription.para: This function converts a 2D Array object into an OpenCV cv::Mat. The conversion process does not involve copying the data; instead, it uses pointers to the original data in the Array. As a result, any modifications to the Array will directly affect the corresponding cv::Mat and vice versa.

simplesect.para: Since this function relies on pointers to the original data, it is crucial for the user to ensure that the Array object remains in scope and valid for the entire lifetime of the cv::Mat. If the Array is destroyed or goes out of scope, the cv::Mat will reference invalid memory, which can lead to undefined behavior.
A cv::Mat object that shares the data with the input Array.

briefdescription.para: Export the array as an OpenEXR image file.

detaileddescription.para: This function saves the array data as an OpenEXR image file. OpenEXR is a high dynamic range (HDR) image file format. The file will be created or overwritten with the name specified by fname.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the OpenEXR file to be created or overwritten.

briefdescription.para: Export the array to a raw binary file.

detaileddescription.para: This function writes the contents of the array to a file in raw binary format. This can be useful for saving the array data to disk or for transferring it between programs. The file will be created or overwritten with the specified fname.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the file to which the array data will be written.

briefdescription.para: Export the array to a numpy binary file.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the file to which the array data will be written.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &fname, int cmap, bool hillshading=false, int depth=CV_8U) const
memberdef.name: to_png

briefdescription.para: Export the array as a PNG image file with a specified colormap and hillshading.

detaileddescription.para: This function saves the array data as a PNG image file. The colors of the image are determined by the specified colormap (cmap), and optional hillshading can be applied to enhance the visual representation of the data. The file will be created or overwritten with the name specified by fname.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the PNG file to be created or overwritten.
The colormap to be used for visualizing the data. Refer to cmap for available options.
Boolean flag to enable or disable hillshading. If true, hillshading will be applied.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string &fname, int depth=CV_8U, float vmin=0.f, float vmax=0.f) const
memberdef.name: to_png_grayscale

briefdescription.para: Export the array as a grayscale PNG image file with specified bit depth.

detaileddescription.para: This function saves the array data as a grayscale PNG image file. The bit depth of the image is determined by the depth parameter, allowing for either 8-bit (0-255) or 16-bit (0-65535) grayscale values. The resulting image will be created or overwritten with the name specified by fname.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the PNG file to be created or overwritten.
The bit depth of the PNG image. Default is 8-bit (CV_8U). Use CV_16U for 16-bit depth if higher precision is needed.

briefdescription.para: Export the array as a TIFF image file.

detaileddescription.para: This function saves the array data as a TIFF (Tagged Image File Format) image file. The file will be created or overwritten with the name specified by fname.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the TIFF file to be created or overwritten.

briefdescription.para: Export the array as a 16-bit raw file for Unity terrain import.

detaileddescription.para: This function saves the array data as a raw binary file with 16-bit depth. The resulting file is suitable for use with Unity terrain import formats. The file will be created or overwritten with the name specified by fname.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the raw file to be created or overwritten.

briefdescription.para: Return the unique elements of the array.

detaileddescription.para: This function extracts and returns a vector containing all unique values present in the array. The values are sorted in ascending order.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> A vector of unique values found in the array.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the multiplication operator for scalar multiplication.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to multiply.
The Array to multiply with.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after multiplication.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the division operator for scalar division.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to divide by.
The Array to divide by.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after division.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the addition operator for scalar addition.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to add.
The Array to add.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after addition.

briefdescription.para: Overloads the subtraction operator for scalar subtraction.

parameterdescription.para: The scalar value to subtract.
The Array to subtract from.

simplesect.para: Array The resulting Array after subtraction.

briefdescription.para: Array class, helper to manipulate 2D float array with "(i, j)" indexing.
